claims.text: Nicola Tesla's 1901 Warden Cliffe tower was built to transmit wireless energy worldwide, not for radio signals.

evidence.quote:
in 1901 Nicola Tesla built Warden
Cliffe the 190 ft tower on Long Island
wasn

claims.rationale: Well-documented historical fact: Wardenclyffe Tower's wireless power transmission goal is part of Tesla's public record.

claims.text: JP Morgan withdrew his financing from Tesla's Wardenclyffe project when he realized Tesla intended to provide free electricity worldwide.

evidence.quote:
JP Morgan and initially funded
the project but withdrew when he
realized Tesla wanted to provide free
electricity worldwide

claims.rationale: Commonly repeated narrative; the 'free electricity' framing is somewhat apocryphal but Morgan's withdrawal is documented.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] presupposes that the Wardenclyffe project existed and had Morgan funding

claims.text: When Tesla died in 1943 the US government moved almost immediately to seize his research and papers.

evidence.quote:
when Tesla died in 1943 the US
government responded fast his body was
still warm

claims.rationale: The 1943 Office of Alien Property seizure of Tesla's papers is widely documented.

claims.text: Dr. John Trump's NDRC review concluded Tesla's research contained nothing of value — a conclusion the host contends was false.

evidence.quote:
Dr Trump also a National Defense
research Committee Member said Tesla's
research contained nothing valuable

claims.rationale: Trump's 1943 review is real; whether it accurately reflected Tesla's work is genuinely contested. The host explicitly calls the review's conclusion false.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] presupposes the government seized the research in the first place

claims.text: After Tesla's death the US military secretly continued his particle-beam research through classified programs Project Nick (1945) and DARPA's Project Seesaw (1958).

evidence.quote:
some research went to write
Patterson Air Force Base under project
Nick

DARPA launched project seesaw in
1958 like project Nick

claims.rationale: Project Nick and Project Seesaw are documented government programs; the causal link to Tesla's specific research is the host's narrative and less firmly established.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] if the military kept researching Tesla's work then Trump's 'no value' conclusion was misleading

claims.text: HARP in Alaska is a direct descendant of physicist Bernard Eastland's 1987 ionospheric-heating patent, which itself built on Tesla's unrealized wireless-power concepts.

evidence.quote:
in 1987 physicist Bernard Eastland
filed a patent titled method and
apparatus

claims.rationale: Eastland's patents and DARPA/Air Force funding of HARP are public record. The Tesla-lineage framing is more interpretive.

claims.text: Eastland's patent explicitly describes military applications — destroying missiles, disrupting communications, modifying weather, and deflecting missile trajectories — contradicting any purely-research framing of the facility.

evidence.quote:
applications uh discussed in the
patents included uh destroying missiles
Communications control and disruption

claims.rationale: The patent language is public record; the inference that this reveals HARP's real purpose is the host's reading.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] if the underlying patent describes weapons uses, then HARP inherits those capabilities

claims.text: Bernard Eastland confirmed the military's weapons intent for HARP before his 2007 death, calling HARP 'the perfect first step toward a plan like mine.'

evidence.quote:
harp is the
perfect first step toward a plan like
mine

claims.rationale: Quote attributed to Eastland appears in multiple Why-Files-adjacent sources; not independently fact-checked by me, but plausible given his own patent framing.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] the inventor's own testimony confirms the weapons intent inferred from his patent

claims.text: Although HARP's 3.6 MW is tiny compared to natural weather systems, chaos-theoretic leverage at the right place and time could let HARP trigger large weather or seismic events.

evidence.quote:
designers understood Chaos
Theory they didn't need to match
Nature

claims.rationale: The butterfly-effect framing is speculative; no peer-reviewed evidence that HARP energy inputs can reliably drive macro-scale weather or tectonic outcomes.

claims.text: Major earthquakes including Haiti 2010, Sichuan 2008 and Fukushima 2011 coincided with ionospheric anomalies correlated with HARP operations, suggesting HARP may have played a role.

evidence.quote:
January 2nd 2020 a
devastating 7.0 magnitude earthquake
struck Haiti

claims.rationale: Correlation between seismic events and ionospheric anomalies is real; the causal HARP hypothesis is fringe and not supported by seismological consensus.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] if chaos-leveraged HARP can trigger disasters then specific earthquake correlations are evidence

claims.text: HARP can generate ELF waves in the same frequency range as human brain waves, making electromagnetic mind-control-style effects at scale technologically plausible.

evidence.quote:
harp can generate waves in
exactly the same range as human brain
waves

claims.rationale: HARP-generated ELF is real; frequency-following response is real; but population-scale neural influence from HARP distances lacks peer-reviewed support.

claims.text: Correlations between HARP operations and natural disasters do not prove HARP caused them — earthquakes and severe weather existed long before HARP.

evidence.quote:
correlations exist between harp
operations in certain disasters but
correlation isn't causation

claims.rationale: Textbook epistemological principle; the host states it explicitly as the verdict-section caveat.

dependencies.rationale: [undercuts] host himself notes causation is not established from correlation
[undercuts] same principle undercuts the chaos-leveraged causation claim

claims.text: HARP is only a scientific research facility — military has no weapons program here.

evidence.quote:
Forma darer employees
have said the most sensitive equipment
was removed

claims.rationale: Host presents this as the official/public narrative to reject; Eastland's patent and DARPA funding suggest a broader purpose.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] cannot simultaneously be research-only and patent-documented weapons platform
[debunks] Eastland's own statement debunks the research-only framing
